name: SPM marine oil peripheral artery disease and healthy-volunteer study
summary: A short-course enriched marine oil study in healthy volunteers and people with symptomatic peripheral artery disease, with lipid mediator, leukocyte phenotype, and macrophage gene-expression endpoints.
population: Healthy volunteers and participants with symptomatic peripheral artery disease; the publication reports 10 healthy volunteers and 10 PAD participants, while the registry records 30 actual enrollees across PAD, OA, and healthy groups

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Resolution Pathway and Tissue Repair because they can show whether the intervention changes biomarkers, mechanistic endpoints, and function in Healthy volunteers and participants with symptomatic peripheral artery disease; the publication reports 10 healthy volunteers and 10 PAD participants, while the registry records 30 actual enrollees across PAD, OA, and healthy groups. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
